extracted.brandBackground: Offers franchises for the operation of a domestic referral agency providing caregiver search, recruitment, and placement services to the elderly.

leadership.name: Steve Everhart
leadership.role: Chief Executive Officer and Managing Member
leadership.background: CEO and Managing Member since formation in December 2022, and founder of predecessor HHI in 2011.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides long-term brand continuity and executive leadership.

leadership.name: Tony Fulton
leadership.background: President since May 2023, and founder of Guardian Angels Homecare since 2003.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings extensive hands-on senior care franchise experience.

leadership.name: Julie-Ann Parrott
leadership.background: COO since January 2023, and co-founder of RetireEASE Senior Services.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees daily operations with over 22 years of senior care experience.

leadership.name: Mike McLain
leadership.background: CGO since January 2023, and President of Altus Recruiting since 1996.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads franchise development and recruitment strategies.

item19.notes: The represented franchisees were selected from the top performing 50th percentile of all franchisees launched between 2022 and 2025.

Brochures, business cards, sponsorships, and networking.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by litigation only in Nevada.

operationalRisks.title: Sales Performance Required
operationalRisks.description: Failure to maintain minimum sales performance levels can lead to termination.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Payments
operationalRisks.description: Minimum royalty and marketing fee payments are required regardless of sales levels starting three months after signing.

financialCondition.auditorName: Edward A. Rose, Jr., CPA, PC

extracted.conceptRationale: The franchise operates as a domestic referral agency matching caregivers with clients, which does not fit standard food, retail, or fitness categories.
extracted.staffingRationale: The business is operated from a home office with no initial employees anticipated during the first few months.

riskReasons: Pro forma is built on only 1 franchised location — a very thin sample; treat the modeled franchisee economics as low-confidence.
A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $122,000 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $240,171/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: The franchise operates as a domestic referral agency matching caregivers with clients, which does not fit standard food, retail, or fitness categories.

benchmark.label: General franchise (uncategorized)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Cost of goods and labor are not disclosed in any FDD — model them explicitly before trusting any margin figure.
benchmark.rampNote: Assume 6–18 months to a mature run-rate.
considerations: Budget COGS and labor explicitly — neither is in the FDD, and together they usually run 45–65% of sales.
Use the Item 20 franchisee list to validate real operating costs and ramp before committing capital.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 8–20% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

insights.staffingNote: Flagged as an lightly staffed model from the FDD, so labor is adjusted down to 10–21% — the 20–35% category band assumes a staffed venue. Confirm actual staffing with Item 20 franchisees.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: lightly staffed · ≈ 6.9–14.6 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “General franchise (uncategorized)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Read as lightly staffed from the FDD, which lowers the labor band applied below.
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 6–12% category band.
Estimated at the 15–35% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 10–21% lightly staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. Hallmark Homecare, Inc. guarantees the franchisor's performance, which partly backstops this.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: elevated unit closures (7 in the last year, about 12% of the system)
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
the parent guarantees the franchisor's performance
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
